title: Running rootless container via REST api fails
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

When I create a container using a rootless REST api and then try and run it, it fails with errors trying to create /var/run/netns.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. podman system service -t 0

2. cat > def.json << __EOM__
{
    "command": ["ls"],
    "image": "fedora:latest",
    "name": "test"
}
__EOM__

3. curl -d @def.json -H "Content-Type: application/json" --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock http://localhost/v1.24/libpod/containers/create

[this seems to work and returns a container id]

4. curl -X POST --unix-socket $XDG_RUNTIME_DIR/podman/podman.sock http://localhost/v1.24/libpod/containers/test/start

**Describe the results you received:**

{"cause":"mkdir /var/run/netns: permission denied","message":"cannot create /var/run/netns: mkdir /var/run/netns: permission denied","response":500}

**Describe the results you expected:**

Container to successfully start

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.9.3
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.14.2
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```

  cpus: 8

  eventLogger: file

  idMappings:

    - container_id: 0

  kernel: 5.6.15-300.fc32.x86_64

  ociRuntime:

      +SYSTEMD +SELINUX +APPARMOR +CAP +SECCOMP +EBPF +YAJL
  os: linux

  slirp4netns:

      SLIRP_CONFIG_VERSION_MAX: 2
  swapFree: 1788424192
```

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
bare metal

comments.body: I think that bit is no longer necessary on 2.0, FYI - if not explicitly set, we will choose a sane default

Thanks.

It took me a while to figure out particularly because it's --network on the
command line and netns in the json, and netns isn't documented in the
swagger at http://docs.podman.io/en/latest/_static/api.html


On Fri, Jun 19, 2020 at 6:33 AM Matthew Heon <notifications@github.com>
wrote:

> set, we will choose a sane default
